steps: Amoxicillin PO 40-45 mg/kg/dose every 12 h x 5 days
Benzylpenicillin IV 50,000 IU/kg every 6 h + Gentamicin IV 7.5 mg/kg once daily
If no improvement at 48 h, switch to Ceftriaxone IV 50 mg/kg once daily
Inhaled salbutamol 2 puffs via spacer Q4-6H PRN
Prednisolone 2 mg/kg PO OD x 3 days
Review trigger avoidance and inhaler technique
Return if: requiring >4-hourly reliever, night waking, exercise intolerance
Nebulised salbutamol 2.5 mg Q20 min x 3 doses then Q1-4H
Prednisolone 2 mg/kg PO OD x 3-5 days (max 60 mg/day)
Oxygen to maintain SpO2 ≥94%
Ipratropium bromide 0.25 mg nebulised with first 3 salbutamol doses
Monitor: SpO2, RR, HR, wheeze severity, PEFR if able
Nebulised salbutamol 2.5 mg continuously or Q15-20 min
Ipratropium bromide 0.25 mg with each salbutamol dose x 3 doses
Prednisolone 2 mg/kg PO/IV OD (max 60 mg/day)
Oxygen to maintain SpO2 ≥94%
IV Magnesium sulphate 40 mg/kg (max 2 g) over 20 min
IV fluids — maintain hydration
Monitor for fatigue, silent chest, rising CO2 — prepare for ventilation
Admit to HDU/ICU

investigations.name: Chest X-ray
investigations.indication: severe, atypical, or if apnoeic episodes

steps: Supportive care — may be managed at home if feeding and SpO2 normal
Nasal suctioning with saline drops before feeds
Encourage small frequent feeds to maintain hydration
Return if: feeding <50% normal, apnoeic pauses, severe respiratory distress
Admit for observation and supportive care
Oxygen to maintain SpO2 ≥90% (≥92% if cyanotic heart disease)
Nasal suctioning PRN — especially before feeds
NG/IV fluids if unable to feed adequately (80% maintenance)
Trial of nebulised salbutamol only if strong family history of atopy
Admit to HDU/ICU
Oxygen to maintain SpO2 ≥90%
Nasal CPAP if severe respiratory distress or apnoeic spells
IV fluids — 80% maintenance
No routine bronchodilators or corticosteroids
Monitor: SpO2 continuous, RR, HR, conscious level, feeding ability Q2-4H
Single dose Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg PO (max 16 mg)
No stridor at rest — can be discharged if caretaker reliable
Return if: stridor at rest develops, increased work of breathing
Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg PO/IV stat (max 16 mg)
Nebulised Adrenaline (1:1000) 0.5 mL/kg (max 5 mL) if stridor at rest
Observe for minimum 4 h after Adrenaline
Admit if stridor persists after 4 h or requires repeated Adrenaline
Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg IV stat (max 16 mg)
Nebulised Adrenaline (1:1000) 0.5 mL/kg — may repeat Q1-2H
Helium-oxygen mixture (Heliox) if available
Prepare for intubation if deteriorating — call anaesthesia early
Admit to ICU
Maintain upright position — do not lay flat
DO NOT attempt blind finger sweep
Call ENT/anaesthesia for emergency bronchoscopy
Oxygen via face mask if SpO2 <92%
Prepare for rigid bronchoscopy: fasting/NBM
If complete obstruction and deteriorating: Heimlich manoeuvre (>1 yr) or back blows + chest thrusts (<1 yr)
Post-bronchoscopy: CXR to confirm removal, monitor for complications (oedema, pneumothorax, infection)
Intensive phase: RHZE daily x 2 months (Rifampicin 15 mg/kg, INH 10 mg/kg, Pyrazinamide 35 mg/kg, Ethambutol 20 mg/kg)
Continuation phase: RH daily x 4 months (Rifampicin 15 mg/kg, INH 10 mg/kg)
Directly Observed Therapy (DOT) strongly recommended
Monitor LFTs at baseline, 2 weeks, and monthly
Same RHZE regimen as non-severe
If TB meningitis/miliary: add Dexamethasone 0.6 mg/kg/day x 4 weeks with taper
Extend continuation phase to 7 months (total 9 months for TB meningitis)
IV ATT if unable to tolerate oral
Monitor for IRIS if HIV co-infected
Nutritional rehabilitation — high protein diet

investigations.name: Chest X-ray PA (or AP if upright)
Chest CT
investigations.indication: if complex, recurrent, or to guide management

steps: Small pneumothorax (<20%) without respiratory distress: observe with high-flow oxygen
Repeat CXR in 4-6 h to confirm stability
Monitor: SpO2 continuous, RR, HR Q4H
Needle decompression: 2nd ICS midclavicular line with 14G/16G cannula
Chest tube insertion: 4th-5th ICS midaxillary line (size 12-16F for children)
Connect to underwater seal drainage
CXR to confirm tube position and lung re-expansion
Monitor: chest drain output, SpO2, RR, BP Q1H
Emergency needle decompression if tension pneumothorax suspected (tracheal deviation, distended neck veins, hypotension)
Immediate chest tube insertion
IV fluids if hypotensive
High-flow oxygen
Monitor: SpO2 continuous, BP, HR, tracheal position, chest drain output hourly
Prepare for thoracotomy if persistent air leak >5-7 days
